trustGraphPage.whyP1: In a world of noise, credentials, and self-promotion, how do you know who to collaborate with? Traditional platforms rely on ratings, reviews, or opaque algorithms. changethegame takes a different approach: a polymorphic attestation system where real people vouch for real contributions.
trustGraphPage.whyP2: The Open Trust Graph replaces popularity metrics with meaningful, verifiable trust signals. It answers the question: "Who has actually worked with this person, guild, or organisation — and what was the experience?"
trustGraphPage.howIntro: Trust is expressed through TrustEdges — directed attestations from one node to another. Nodes can be Profiles, Guilds, Quests, Services, Companies, or Territories.
howItems: Anyone can give a trust attestation to another entity they've interacted with
Each edge carries a score (1–5), optional tags, a note, and an evidence URL
Edges can be public, network-visible, or private
A global weighted score is calculated: Base 1 + (score × 0.2)
Freshness matters: edges older than 24 months receive a 0.8× decay multiplier
Trust is renewable: after 12 months, you're prompted to confirm or retract your attestation
trustGraphPage.antiGamingIntro: Trust must be earned, not gamed. The system enforces several constraints to maintain integrity:
antiGamingItems: Maximum 3 public trust edges per user per week
6-month cooldown between the same node pair
Reciprocal trust given within 48h without evidence receives a 50% XP reduction
Periodic renewal: unconfirmed edges are marked as outdated after 18 months
trustGraphPage.rewardsIntro: Trust attestations feed both the XP reputation system and the credit economy:
rewardsItems: Public edges award specialised XP (Stewardship, Maker, Community, Tech Commons, etc.)
Monthly XP cap of 40 per category — overflow is stored and released gradually
Credit bonuses for high-quality attestations (evidence-backed, marked 'Useful')
Stewards receiving trust in their domain earn additional credits
trustGraphPage.opennessP1: The trust graph is designed to be open and portable. Public attestations are available via a public API endpoint compatible with JSON-LD and future Verifiable Credentials standards. This means trust data can be verified and consumed by external systems — no lock-in.
opennessItems: Pagination, filtering by tag, edge type, or territory
Only public + active edges are ever exposed
trustGraphPage.platformIntro: Trust signals are woven into every part of the experience:
platformItems: Dedicated 'Trust' tab on every entity page (profile, guild, company, territory…)
Trust badges and scores in search results and mini-cards
'Top Trusted Members' ranking within guilds and territories
A multi-step 'Give Trust' wizard with real-time limit validation
Trust renewal dashboard in your Network settings

aboutPage.purposeText: changethegame exists to help humans collaborate, create, and regenerate the world. We blend collective intelligence, creativity, territories, and AI-powered agents into a single ecosystem where anyone can launch quests, join guilds, offer services, and activate their region or community.
valuesList: Creativity as a force for transformation
AI as an amplifier of human agency
whatYouCanDoList: Create or join guilds and collectives
Learn with courses, sessions, and events
Collaborate with AI agents dedicated to each unit
aboutPage.trustText: Reputation on changethegame isn't based on likes or self-reported CVs. It's built on the Open Trust Graph — a system where real people attest to real contributions. Trust edges carry scores, evidence, and tags, and they decay over time to stay relevant. This ensures that the people, guilds, and organisations you see have been genuinely vouched for.
aboutPage.visionText: A world where people, organizations, and territories collaborate fluidly to build a regenerative, creative and inclusive future — one quest at a time.

howItWorksPage.step1Text: Pick your Houses (interests), choose your Territory, and define your persona (Impact, Creative, or Hybrid).
howItWorksPage.step2Text: Quests are missions, projects, or creative endeavors. You can:
howItWorksPage.step3Text: Guilds (or collectives) are communities united around shared themes. They offer:
howItWorksPage.step4Text: Turn your skills into bookable sessions. Includes:
howItWorksPage.step5Text: Companies represent your professional world. Pods are small, temporary groups created for specific quests or collaborations.
howItWorksPage.step6Text: Territories anchor action in the real world. Houses represent topics, themes, interests and expertise.
howItWorksPage.step7Text: Every guild, quest, company or territory comes with an AI agent that:
howItWorksPage.step8Text: Earn XP from quests, collaborations and contributions. Earn credits from system activities or purchases. Use credits to fund quests, proposals, or services.
howItWorksPage.step9Text: Your reputation is built through trust attestations — not vanity metrics. When someone vouches for you, they create a TrustEdge with a score, tags, and optional evidence. These edges are visible on your profile and feed into your XP and credit rewards. Trust decays over time and must be periodically renewed, keeping the ecosystem honest and up to date.
step9Items: Give and receive trust attestations across all entity types
Scores from 1–5 with tags, notes, and evidence links
Anti-gaming: weekly limits, cooldowns, and reciprocal penalties
Public edges are available via an open API (JSON-LD compatible)

manifestoPage.believe1: We believe people can create extraordinary things when they are seen, supported and connected.
manifestoPage.believe2: We believe human collectives can work with traditional organisations.
manifestoPage.believe3: We believe creativity and impact are not separate.
manifestoPage.believe4: We believe territories are alive and deserve to be understood.
manifestoPage.believe5: We believe collaboration is a form of beauty.
manifestoPage.believe6: We believe technology should amplify the human, not replace it.
manifestoPage.believe7: We believe ownership should be shared.
manifestoPage.believe8: We believe the future is built one quest at a time.
manifestoPage.believe9: We believe living systems should be involved in territorial governance.
manifestoPage.can4: and transform the places they live in.
manifestoPage.movement: This is our movement, our coop-like venture, our little rebellion.
manifestoPage.notAttention: We're not here to take attention.
manifestoPage.powerBack: We're here to give people their power back.

cooperativePage.heroTitle: A platform owned by the people who use it.
cooperativePage.heroDesc: changethegame is not just a tool. It is a shared infrastructure — built, governed, and owned collectively by its members. We chose a coop-like structure because the tools we use to collaborate should belong to the people who collaborate.
cooperativePage.memberOwnedDesc: Humans who contribute shape what gets built. Ownership is earned through participation, not just capital.
cooperativePage.democraticGovDesc: Decisions are made collectively. Governance weight is a hybrid of XP reputation and shareholding — not money alone.
cooperativePage.surplusReinvestedDesc: Annual surplus is split across reinvestment, shareholders, treasury, and solidarity — never extracted for private gain.
cooperativePage.threeShareClassesDesc: Each class reflects a different form of commitment — from strategic stewardship to day-to-day participation.
cooperativePage.classADesc: Class A members are the foundational stewards of changethegame. They hold deep governance rights, participate in strategic decisions, and take long-term responsibility for the mission. Their involvement goes beyond investment — they co-define the direction of the venture.
classARights: Priority input on the product roadmap
cooperativePage.classAAccessNote: Access is by application only. We look for long-term alignment, not just capital.
cooperativePage.classBSubtitle: Active participation · Open to all Humans
cooperativePage.classBDesc: Class B is open to any Human on the platform who wants to go beyond using the tool — and actually own a piece of it. Stewards support the project financially, influence its direction, and participate in governance proportional to their XP level and contribution history.
cooperativePage.classBGovNote: Governance rights scale with XP: levels 5–8 unlock voting, 9–12 proposals, 13–15 council eligibility.
cooperativePage.joinClassB: Join as a Class B Steward
cooperativePage.classCDesc: Class C shares are designed for institutions, foundations, public bodies, and long-term aligned organizations that want to participate structurally in the platform's governance and ecosystem development. Not investors — infrastructure partners committed to mission resilience.
cooperativePage.classCNote: Not publicly tradable. Bound by mission protection clause. Requires strategic alignment review.
cooperativePage.howGovDesc: Governance weight is a hybrid of XP (non-transferable reputation earned through activity) and shareholding. XP is the primary gating mechanism — money alone does not grant influence.
cooperativePage.xpNeverDecays: XP never decays and cannot be purchased. A +10% XP bonus applies to contributions made outside a member's primary territory, incentivising cross-ecosystem activity.
cooperativePage.surplusDesc: Annual surplus is never extracted. It is redistributed across four pools defined by member governance.
cooperativePage.shareholdersDesc: Dividend distribution to all share classes
contributionItems: Infrastructure & hosting for the global platform
New features shaped by members' needs
cooperativePage.ctaTitle: Ready to co-own the platform you use?
cooperativePage.ctaDesc: Join as a Class B Steward — open to all active Humans.

useCasesPage.title: 10 Use Cases
useCasesPage.subtitle: How changethegame supports freelancers, organizations, collectives, territories, ecosystem builders, citizens & communities.

cases.title: The Freelancer Who Wants to Shine
cases.description: A freelance designer or strategist builds a living profile enhanced by AI, presents services, past quests, causes & territories, receives reviews & XP, appears in talent searches, and gets paid in fiat or earns credits through contributions.

cases.title: The Public Actor Looking for the Right Talent
cases.description: A city, region, NGO, or company publishes open quests, searches talent by domain, cause and territory, creates temporary task forces mixing freelancers and guild members, and tracks progress & funding centrally.

cases.title: A Collective Organizing Its Actions
cases.description: A community, choir, DAO, or citizen group creates a guild to access rituals & online gatherings, internal and public quests, shared task management, a co-financing pool (fiat + credits), and partnerships with other guilds.

cases.title: Crowdfunded Quests for Collective Action
cases.description: A guild launches a regeneration project — mapping abandoned buildings, training volunteers, designing composting systems. Contributions flow in fiat or credits, volunteers earn XP, admins define milestones & roles, and AI assists in writing the project.

cases.title: Personal Task Management Inside Every Quest
cases.description: Every user creates their own task list, links tasks to quests, guilds, services or territories, gets AI suggestions to refine tasks, and drags tasks into quest timelines. A personal productivity system embedded inside a cooperative action ecosystem.

cases.title: Territorial Diagnosis for Decision Makers
cases.description: A local authority or changemaker uses the Territory Analyst AI to surface vulnerabilities, emerging opportunities, active actors & guilds, possible quests to launch, and recommended partners. This helps territories design evidence-based strategies.

cases.title: Real-World Community Activation
cases.description: Users with shared motivations meet physically, organize walk-throughs, workshops, hackathons and rituals, sync events with guild calendars, and create local micro-communities inside larger guilds. A bridge between digital commons and real territory life.

cases.title: Multi-Currency Value Exchange
cases.description: Participants interact using fiat for formal services, credits for contributions and symbolic exchanges, XP to grow reputation and unlock governance rights, and A/B shares to join long-term cooperative ownership. A regenerative economy powering a cooperative platform.

cases.title: AI Co-Pilots Supporting Each Persona
cases.description: Specialized assistants serve every need: Territory Analyst for deep-dives, Community Strategist for engagement, Grant Writer for funding proposals, Content Creator for bios and pages, Coach for goal setting. Each generates XP and uses credits, reinforcing engagement loops.

cases.title: Collective Governance of a Digital Commons
cases.description: Users who accumulate XP vote on platform improvements, participate in micro-governance inside their guild, join circles steering territories or program tracks, propose new rules or shared resources, and eventually hold cooperative shares for deeper involvement.

useCasesPage.whoForDesc: Across skills, missions, governance, funding, territorial analysis and real-world activation — changethegame is where human collaboration meets AI-augmented impact.

productVisionPage.title: Product Vision 2026
productVisionPage.subtitle: The cooperative platform that turns people, communities and territories into engines of transformation.
productVisionPage.ambitionP1: changethegame is a global digital cooperative designed for people, organizations and territories who want to act, collaborate and regenerate their ecosystems.
productVisionPage.ambitionP2: It is not a social network. It is not a marketplace. It is not a project management tool.
productVisionPage.ambitionP3: changethegame is an <1>action infrastructure</1> — where skills, missions, communities, territories, resources and collective intelligence turn into real-world impact.
visionItems: Individuals showcase talents, stories and contributions
Organizations find the right people and launch missions
Territories become readable, understandable and actionable
Value circulates through multiple systems (fiat, credits, XP, shares)
Everyone contributes to a shared digital commons
AI acts as a co-pilot for transformation

pillars.title: Living Profiles — Amplified by AI
pillars.description: Each user owns a living profile: skills, territories, causes, services, quests completed, XP level and community involvement.
aiFeatures: Generate profile from CV / LinkedIn / text
Identify ideal quests, roles or guilds
Rewrite bios, pitches and project descriptions
pillars.goal: Reduce friction, increase visibility, enhance impact.

pillars.title: Guilds — Communities Designed for Action
pillars.description: Autonomous communities with rituals & gatherings, members & roles, internal quests, partnerships, collective funding, shared knowledge and distributed governance.
pillars.goal: Structure communities so they can act with clarity, agency and collective intelligence.

pillars.title: Quests — The Engine of Value and Impact
pillars.description: Professional missions, community challenges, territorial actions, rituals, learning pathways, creative explorations and transition projects.
aiFeatures: Funded via fiat, credits, XP sponsorship, guild or territorial funds
AI co-writes, analyzes and recommends quests
pillars.goal: Help individuals, organizations and territories turn needs into action.

pillars.title: Living Territories — Intelligence for Regeneration
pillars.description: Every territory has a dynamic portrait, AI-powered ecosystem analysis, maps of skills and actors, a territory feed of needs and signals, and territorial quests.
pillars.goal: Give local actors a live intelligence layer to guide transformation.

pillars.title: The Social Graph — Purposeful & Real-World Anchored
pillars.description: Connect people through shared causes, skills, place-based involvement, guild membership and project participation.
pillars.goal: Weave meaningful networks of practice, purpose and mutual support.

pillars.title: A Cooperative Multi-Economy
pillars.description: Value circulates through four complementary systems: fiat currency, credits (internal currency with slow demurrage), XP (reputation with 15 bio-inspired levels), and cooperative shares A/B/C.
aiFeatures: Credits earned through contributions, unlock tools & quests
XP grants governance rights with territory multipliers
pillars.goal: Align money, recognition, cooperation and governance.

productVisionPage.aiRoleTitle: The Role of AI — A Constant Co-pilot
productVisionPage.aiRoleGoal: Augment human capacities, not replace them.
productVisionPage.promiseDesc: To reinvent how humans collaborate to transform their ecosystems. A space where:

revenuePage.overviewP: The platform operates on four distinct economic layers, each serving a specific purpose. These layers are intentionally separated to avoid confusion and ensure alignment between ownership, value exchange, utility, and recognition.
revenuePage.creditsDesc: Internal utility units for platform features.
revenuePage.shareholdingP: Shareholding refers to legal participation in ownership or governance, governed by formal legal statutes. It may grant governance rights depending on the legal structure.
shareholdingItems: Is not automatically linked to XP, credits, or usage.
Does not guarantee financial returns unless legally defined.
revenuePage.shareholdingCallout:
Shareholding ≠ XP
Shareholding ≠ Credits
Shareholding ≠ Guaranteed Income
revenuePage.monetaryP: Users may generate revenue through the platform via:
monetaryPrincipleItems: No claim of ownership over user contracts.
Compliance with financial and tax regulations.
revenuePage.monetaryNote: Money transactions are external currency flows and are independent from XP or Credits.
revenuePage.creditsP: Credits are internal, non-convertible coordination units that circulate within the ecosystem.
creditsCoreItems: Subject to 1% monthly demurrage (same rate as $CTG tokens)
Cannot be withdrawn, traded, or exchanged externally
Do not represent ownership or financial return
revenuePage.demurrageP: Credits are subject to a 1.5% monthly redistribution rate. Inactive balances gradually return to the Platform Treasury, which reinvests them into collective quests, territorial initiatives, and ecosystem-wide development. This mechanism encourages circulation and prevents concentration of coordination power.
revenuePage.creditsCallout: Credits ≠ Money · Credits ≠ Investment · Credits ≠ Equity
revenuePage.creditsCalloutSub: Credits are a coordination & reciprocity tool, not a financial asset.
revenuePage.learnMoreCredits: Learn more about the Credit Economy →
xpPropItems: Cannot be converted into credits or money.
revenuePage.sustainabilityP: The platform operates on a hybrid sustainability model:
sustainabilityItems: Transaction fees — applied only when value is created.
revenuePage.governanceP: The four economic layers are kept deliberately separate. The following safeguards apply:

creditEconomyPage.title: Hybrid Sovereign Economy
creditEconomyPage.subtitle: Understanding the five value layers that power the ecosystem: fiat currency, Coins, $CTG contribution tokens, collaboration credits, and reputation.
creditEconomyPage.calloutLayers: 💶 Fiat = Real economy · 🟩 Coins = Mission value · 🌱 $CTG = Contribution · 🔷 Credits = Platform fuel · ⭐ XP = Reputation
creditEconomyPage.calloutLayersSub: These five layers are intentionally separated. They do not overlap or convert into each other.
creditEconomyPage.creditsIntro: Credits reward contribution beyond financial exchange. They are a non-convertible internal coordination unit designed to facilitate participation and collaborative activation.
creditsEarnedItems: Small universal monthly mint (all active users)
creditEconomyPage.creditsMustFeel: Credits must feel useful but not financial.
creditEconomyPage.fadeIntro: To prevent hoarding and encourage active circulation, Credits gently fade by {{rate}} per month.
creditEconomyPage.fadeExplain: This mechanism ensures that inactive value returns to active community use through the Ecosystem Treasury.
creditEconomyPage.fadeResult: After 12 months of inactivity: ~{{remaining}} credits remain (≈{{percent}}% redistributed)
creditEconomyPage.fadeCallout: Demurrage is not a penalty — it is a structural incentive to activate and circulate value.
creditEconomyPage.fadeCalloutSub: Active contributors naturally neutralize fade through earning.
creditEconomyPage.treasuryIntro: A portion of platform surplus supports territorial and regenerative initiatives.
creditEconomyPage.treasuryMaySupport: Treasury funds may support: new territorial hubs, residencies, regenerative missions, and collective innovation projects.
creditEconomyPage.treasuryConnects: This connects marketplace success to ecosystem growth.
creditEconomyPage.legalNatureP: Credits are an internal, non-convertible coordination unit used exclusively within the changethegame platform.
creditEconomyPage.legalNoConvert: Credits do not constitute electronic money, do not represent a financial instrument, do not grant ownership, equity, or securities rights, are not redeemable for fiat currency, and cannot be withdrawn or exchanged outside the platform.
creditEconomyPage.legalNoValueP: Credits have no intrinsic monetary value and are not intended to function as currency. The purchase of subscription plans or top-ups grants access to platform features and allocates internal Credits. Such purchases do not constitute the acquisition of a financial asset.
creditEconomyPage.legalDemurrageP: Credits are subject to a monthly redistribution mechanism (currently {{rate}}) applied to inactive balances. This is a structural feature of the internal coordination system, not a fee, penalty, or financial charge. Redistributed Credits are allocated to the Ecosystem Treasury for collective initiatives.
creditEconomyPage.legalAuthorityP: changethegame reserves the right to modify Credit mechanics, adjust redistribution rates, prevent abuse or manipulation, and suspend accounts engaging in exploitative behaviour. All changes will be communicated transparently.
creditEconomyPage.legalSeparationP: All financial transactions involving services, missions, or fundraising are conducted in fiat currency through regulated payment providers. Credits are not linked to financial returns and do not guarantee economic benefit.
creditEconomyPage.legalNotSpeculativeP: changethegame is not a token platform. It is a coordination infrastructure designed to operate within current economic systems while preparing long-term regenerative alternatives. No crypto logic, no token speculation.

governancePage.title: Cooperative Governance
governancePage.subtitle: Changethegame combines marketplace activity with cooperative stewardship.
governancePage.howUnlockedP1: Governance rights are unlocked by XP level, participation history, and shareholding. XP determines your participation tier. Shares increase long-term responsibility.
governancePage.howUnlockedP2: This ensures merit-based legitimacy, long-term alignment, and protection from speculative capture.
governancePage.guildGovP: Guilds operate with configurable membership policies (open, application-based, invite-only), role-based permissions, and AI-assisted decision-making through polls and proposals.
governancePage.territorialActivationP: Territories are activated through quests, guilds, and people. AI agents help identify gaps and opportunities for local collaboration.
governancePage.designPrinciplesP: The governance architecture avoids complex quadratic voting and token-based voting. It is designed for progressive activation — simple today, with infrastructure for deeper participation as the ecosystem matures.

privacyPage.intro: We collect only the data necessary to provide the platform and its collaborative features. We never sell your data. AI agents operate with strict boundaries and unit-specific context.
privacyPage.s1P: We collect information you provide directly (name, email, profile data) and usage data (pages visited, actions taken, device information) to improve the Platform.
privacyPage.s2P: Your data is used to: provide and improve Platform features; personalise your experience; send notifications and digests; process payments; and ensure platform security.
privacyPage.s3Title: 3. AI Agent Usage and Context Limits
privacyPage.s3P: AI agents only access data within their assigned unit (guild, quest, company, territory). They do not have global access to your data or activity across the platform.
privacyPage.s4P: Data within guilds, companies, and quests is isolated to authorized members. Public content is visible to all users; private content is restricted by role-based access.
privacyPage.s5P: We use essential, analytics, and preference cookies. See our Cookie Policy for details.
privacyPage.s6P: We use service providers for payment processing, hosting, and analytics. All operate under strict data protection agreements.
privacyPage.s7P: We retain your data for as long as your account is active. Soft-deleted content is permanently removed after 30 days. You may request full deletion at any time.
privacyPage.s8P: You have the right to access, rectify, delete, or export your personal data. You may also object to processing or request restriction.
privacyPage.s9P: For privacy inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at privacy@changethegame.xyz.

termsPage.s1P: By accessing and using changethegame ("the Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the Platform.
termsPage.s2P: You must provide accurate information when creating an account. You are responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your credentials and for all activities under your account.
termsPage.s3P: You agree not to: post harmful, misleading, or illegal content; harass other users; attempt to manipulate XP or contribution metrics; or circumvent rate limits or platform restrictions.
termsPage.s4P: Content you create on the Platform remains yours. By posting, you grant changethegame a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to display and distribute your content within the Platform.
termsPage.s5P: AI agents may generate summaries, suggestions, and drafts. AI-generated content is provided as-is. You are responsible for reviewing and approving any AI output before publishing or acting on it.
termsPage.s6P: Paid plans, service bookings, and credit purchases are processed through our payment provider. Refund policies apply as described at the time of purchase.
termsPage.s7P: Service providers are responsible for the accuracy of their listings, availability, and fulfillment. changethegame facilitates but does not guarantee service delivery.
termsPage.s8P: Quest participation is voluntary. changethegame is not liable for the outcome of collaborations between users.
termsPage.s9P: Guilds, companies, and pods are self-governed by their members. changethegame provides tools but does not enforce internal governance decisions.
termsPage.s10P: changethegame is provided "as is" without warranties. We are not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the Platform.
termsPage.s11P: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these terms. Users may delete their accounts at any time through settings.
termsPage.s12P: Disputes will be resolved through good-faith communication. For unresolved issues, contact us at legal@changethegame.xyz.

contactPage.reachOutP: Have a question, request or issue? We respond to all messages.
contactPage.successToast: Thank you! We'll get back to you shortly.

analyzeItems: Suggestions for activation and community building
territoryAgentsPage.purposeP: They help territories regenerate intelligently and collaboratively.

pi.noActiveQuests: No active quests at the moment.
pi.greetingSubtext: Describe what you'd like to accomplish and I'll guide you through the platform.
pi.placeholder: Ask Pi... E.g: 'Find a guild about solar energy'
pi.sendHint: Enter to send · Shift+Enter for line break
pi.errorRetry: Sorry, an error occurred. Please try again.
pi.skippedAlternatives: No problem! Here are some other options:
pi.suggestModifyPrompt: Can you modify the previous proposal?
pi.suggestSearchExistingPrompt: Search my existing quests, guilds, and services for something similar
pi.suggestDifferentPrompt: Let me try a different approach
